title: Miconia neocoloradensis
taxonomy: Miconia neocoloradensis is classified as a species within the family Melastomataceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the melastome family. This family is part of the order Myrtales, a large and diverse order of plants that also includes the families Lythraceae and Penaeaceae. The family Melastomataceae is native to the Americas and Southeast Asia, with many species found in tropical and subtropical regions. Miconia neocoloradensis is a rare and endemic species, meaning it is found only in a specific geographic area. The classification of Miconia neocoloradensis provides insight into its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants.
